mistake: Restarted PREFLIGHT assessment mid-workflow when user gave continuation instructions with refinements
why_wrong: User said 'the integration hub is great, but needs sub pages...' - this was continuation guidance (do X then Y), NOT a signal that work was broken. I misinterpreted refinement instructions as investigation trigger.
prevention: Before starting new PREFLIGHT: 1) Check goals-get-subtasks for completion status, 2) Query handoff for what's done, 3) If user gives specific next steps, continue working with adjustments, 4) Pattern: 'X is great, but needs Y' = refinement, NOT 'X is broken, investigate'

mistake: Skipped PREFLIGHT assessment for iteration 8 session. Went directly to work without epistemic baseline, resulting in 'No PREFLIGHT checkpoint found' warning. Cannot calculate learning deltas or calibration accuracy for this session.
why_wrong: CASCADE workflow requires PREFLIGHT → Work → POSTFLIGHT. Skipping PREFLIGHT means: (1) No learning measurement (can't calculate deltas), (2) No calibration score (can't compare predicted vs actual), (3) Incomplete epistemic record. The framework correctly flagged this violation.
prevention: Always start new sessions with PREFLIGHT assessment, even for 'quick' tasks. The 30 seconds to assess baseline saves confusion later and enables proper learning measurement. Could add CLI reminder: 'No PREFLIGHT detected - create one now? [y/n]'
mistake_data: {"mistake": "Skipped PREFLIGHT assessment for iteration 8 session. Went directly to work without epistemic baseline, resulting in 'No PREFLIGHT checkpoint found' warning. mistake: Used PREFLIGHT with wrong session ID. Created session ec42a261..., but submitted PREFLIGHT for ae05e53c.... Result: 'No PREFLIGHT checkpoint found' warning AGAIN. Did exact same type of workflow violation in two consecutive iterations.
why_wrong: Session IDs must match across CASCADE phases. Each session needs its own PREFLIGHT. Submitting PREFLIGHT for a different session means current session has no baseline, cannot calculate deltas. Framework correctly flagged this.
prevention: Always verify session ID matches after creation. Could add to cli_doc_validator: check for session ID consistency in examples. Add --verify-session-id flag that confirms session exists before accepting assessment.

mistake: Implemented memory-compact handler but forgot to add CLI parser and register command
why_wrong: Hooks couldn't call 'empirica memory-compact' because command doesn't exist in CLI
cost_estimate: 2 hours debugging hooks before discovering root cause
prevention: When implementing new commands: 1) Create handler 2) Create parser 3) Register in cli_core.py 4) Add to help categories 5) Test CLI invocation

mistake: Auto-capture system implemented but never integrated into CLI error handling - made this mistake previously
why_wrong: AutoIssueCaptureService exists and gets initialized during session-create, but handle_cli_error() doesn't use it to capture errors automatically
cost_estimate: Multiple sessions debugging CLI errors that could have been auto-captured and handed off
prevention: When adding new infrastructure systems: 1) Implement core service 2) Integrate into ALL relevant call sites 3) Test integration 4) Verify auto-capture is working 5) Document in system prompt
